MINI MINUTES: CROSS DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO HEALTH CARE AND REHAB, INC'S, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The unopposed demurrer to Savior Hospice Care, Inc.'s cross-complaint is sustained with leave to amend.

A proposed order has been submitted as instructed in the tentative ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jan-16-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Jonathan Van Ee THE LAW OFFICES OF JONATHAN VAN EE (415) 938-7694 Attorney for Plaintiff
SAN FRANCISCO HEALTH CARE AND REHAB, INC
JOE SANDBANK, ESQ. Sandbank Law, APC (916) 672-8018 Attorney for Savior Hospice Care, Inc., Trilochan Singh

Hearing is held. ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS SAVIOR HOSPICE CARE INC, TRILOCHAN SINGH'S Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment.

Defendants Savior Hospice Care, Inc. and Trilochan Singh's motion to set aside default and default judgment is granted. The default and default judgment against the moving parties are now vacated and they must re-file their answer and cross-complaint no later than February 3, 2026, which must be accepted for filing nunc pro tunc as of June 9, 2025. Moving defendants are entitled to the relief they seek per CCP 473(d) and CRC 2.259 due to sufficient proof that they attempted to file their answer and cross-complaint on June 9, 2025 and the Court inadvertently failed to accept them for filing. Because the answer and cross-complaint were not filed due to the error of the Court, plaintiff is not entitled to any compensatory costs. This ruling is supported by California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits.

Proposed order submitted.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/HEK)

DEFAULT HEARING (PROVE UP)
Off Calendar Dec-10-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for plaintiff's request for entry of default judgment.

Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Damon Carter; Dept. 505
Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:20am and concluding at 10:28am.

Attorney for Plaintiff Jonathan Van Ee appeared via zoom.
Defendants Savior Hospice Care Inc. and Trilochan Singh not appearing.

Counsel for Plaintiff presents summary of case and an offer of proof.

Court examines Plaintiff's witness STAN STUKOV via zoom and he confirms statements made by counsel.

The matter is submitted.

Based on the documents filed, the statement made by Plaintiff and its Counsel, consideration and good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff San Francisco Health Care and Rehab Inc., and against Defendants Savior Hospice Care Inc. and Trilochan Singh, as follows:

Damages: $5,292.87
Prejudgment Interest: $0
Attorney Fees: $13,260.00
Costs: $665.00

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $19,217.87

Proposed judgment signed and filed this date.
